pattern: Two diagonals form a rectangle exactly when they share a midpoint and an equal length, so group point pairs by (midpoint, length) and combine pairs within a group into candidate rectangles.
hints: A rectangle's two diagonals cross at their common center and are equal in length.
Index every pair of points by its midpoint and squared length; any two pairs sharing both describe a rectangle.
For each group of diagonal pairs with the same center and length, every two of them give a rectangle whose area you compute from the side vectors; keep the minimum.

edge_inputs.description: a tilted square of area two
no four points form a rectangle
too few points for any rectangle
collinear points never make a rectangle

stress.description: small clusters of points biased to occasionally form rectangles
stress.note: unique points over a tight grid so some rectangles appear

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def minAreaFreeRect(self, points):
        import math
        from collections import defaultdict
        groups = defaultdict(list)
        n = len(points)
        for i in range(n):
            for j in range(i + 1, n):
                x1, y1 = points[i]
                x2, y2 = points[j]
                cx, cy = x1 + x2, y1 + y2
                d2 = (x1 - x2) ** 2 + (y1 - y2) ** 2
                groups[(cx, cy, d2)].append((points[i], points[j]))
        best = float('inf')
        for key, pairs in groups.items():
            m = len(pairs)
            for a in range(m):
                for b in range(a + 1, m):
                    (p1, p2) = pairs[a]
                    (p3, p4) = pairs[b]
                    v1 = (p3[0] - p1[0], p3[1] - p1[1])
                    v2 = (p4[0] - p1[0], p4[1] - p1[1])
                    area = math.hypot(*v1) * math.hypot(*v2)
                    best = min(best, area)
        return 0.0 if best == float('inf') else best
